# CTOS Portfolio
# 13 — Crab Behavior Evolution Roadmap

## Purpose

This roadmap focuses exclusively on evolving the CTOS Crab from an animated SVG into a living digital character.

Current State:

Eyes move.

Target State:

The crab behaves like a living system companion.

This roadmap intentionally ignores:

- UI
- Layout
- Content
- SEO
- Architecture

Focus:

Behavior.

---

# Current Assessment

## Visual Quality

8/10

---

## SVG Quality

8/10

---

## Motion Quality

5/10

---

## Character Personality

2/10

---

## Living Character Score

2/10

---

# Problem Statement

Current Behavior:

Idle
↓
Eye Movement
↓
Idle

Result:

The user sees an animated SVG.

Not a character.

---

# Target State

The user should feel:

"The crab noticed me."

Not:

"The crab is animated."

---

# Phase 01
# Full Body Idle System

## Objective

Remove static body behavior.

---

## Add Breathing

Body should expand and contract subtly.

Cycle:

4–7 seconds

Randomized.

---

## Add Leg Adjustments

Every 8–15 seconds.

Randomized.

One leg moves slightly.

Never synchronized.

---

## Add Claw Drift

Small idle claw movement.

Very subtle.

---

## Add Antenna Movement

Slow oscillation.

Independent timing.

---

## Success Criteria

Users can identify life-like behavior without interacting.

---

# Phase 02
# Attention System

## Objective

The crab becomes aware of nearby activity.

---

## Detection Zones

Zone 01

Far

No reaction.

---

Zone 02

Nearby

Eyes track cursor.

---

Zone 03

Approach

Body shifts slightly.

---

Zone 04

Hover

Full awareness.

---

## Success Criteria

The crab notices users before they interact.

---

# Phase 03
# Reaction Chains

## Objective

Replace single animations with behavior sequences.

---

## Example

Current:

Hover
↓
Animation

---

Target:

Hover
↓
Notice
↓
Pause
↓
Inspect
↓
React
↓
Settle

---

## Success Criteria

Every interaction becomes a story.

---

# Phase 04
# Personality Layer

## Objective

Give the crab recognizable traits.

---

## Curious Mode

Looks around.

Observes nodes.

Tracks movement.

---

## Sentinel Mode

Protects systems.

Observes architecture graph.

Signals active nodes.

---

## Playful Mode

Rewards user exploration.

---

## Success Criteria

Users can describe the crab's personality.

---

# Phase 05
# Context Awareness

## Objective

The crab reacts differently depending on what is happening.

---

## Cloudflare

Signal pulse.

---

## Astro

Orbit reaction.

---

## Vue

Reactive ripple.

---

## Proxmox

Infrastructure pulse.

---

## AI Systems

Scan animation.

---

## Success Criteria

Interactions no longer feel generic.

---

# Phase 06
# Hidden Behaviors

## Objective

Create discovery moments.

---

## User Idle

Crab looks left.

---

## Longer Idle

Crab looks right.

---

## Extended Idle

Crab raises claw.

---

## Success Criteria

Users witness unexpected behavior.

---

# Phase 07
# Celebration System

## Objective

Reward meaningful actions.

---

## Open Case Study

Mini celebration.

---

## Open Command Center

Signal animation.

---

## Discover Architecture Explorer

Special reaction.

---

## Success Criteria

Interactions feel rewarding.

---

# Phase 08
# SVG Rigging Architecture

## Required Parts

Eyes

Pupils

Body

Claws

Legs

Antenna

Signals

Particles

---

## Rule

Every part must animate independently.

---

# Phase 09
# State Machine System

Idle

Observe

Aware

Inspect

Signal

Celebrate

Return

---

## Rule

No animation should trigger directly.

All interactions should transition through states.

---

# Phase 10
# Claude-Level Benchmark

The goal is not to copy Claude.

The goal is to achieve the same perception quality.

---

Visitors should feel:

- Curious
- Delighted
- Rewarded
- Guided

---

# Performance Rules

Target:

60 FPS

---

Avoid:

Heavy SVG calculations.

Continuous layout shifts.

Large animation libraries.

---

# Success Metrics

Current:

Animated SVG

---

Target:

Living Digital Companion

---

Current Score:

2/10

---

Target Score:

9/10

---

# Final Vision

The CTOS Crab should become the most memorable element of the portfolio.

Not because it moves.

Because it behaves.

The user should remember:

"That crab felt alive."

More than:

"That website had animations."
